title: narcissus papyraceus var. niveus
intro: Narcissus papyraceus var. niveus, also known as the white paper narcissus, is a type of plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its white, bell-shaped flowers and papery leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of white paper narcissus include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and soothe skin irritations. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by drying the flowers and leaves, and then steeping them in hot water.
